mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p-value below 0.05, or rolling Rank IC exceeds 0.05 positively.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces corporate profit margins and consumer spending growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in existing home sales and housing starts signal a collapsing housing demand cycle, which transmits to broader consumer spending and equity valuations within one month.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns, with forward return distribution statistically indistinguishable from baseline periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase indicates strengthening corporate output and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive coefficient flips sign in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 20-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 205.0, inflation expectations force central banks to tighten policy aggressively, compressing equity multiples and producing negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 205.0 coincide with positive equity returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the threshold effect vanishes after controlling for monetary policy lags.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling existing home sales indicates a structural shift in consumer spending away from durable goods, weakening financial sector earnings and equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative returns, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in industrial production and payroll employment during a mid-cycle expansion signals robust capacity utilization and wage growth, fueling short-term equity momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: Joint expansion in INDPRO and PAYEMS yields negative forward returns during mid-cycle phases, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample tests.
